raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California's AB 2115 will transform the state from having the most restrictive methadone laws to one of the most accessible.",
    "Methadone is an effective medication for easing opioid withdrawal symptoms.",
    "Bureaucratic barriers to methadone treatment in California have contributed to an increase in opioid overdoses since 2019.",
    "Current regulations in California regarding methadone treatment exceed federal guidelines.",
    "AB 2115 allows doctors to prescribe up to 72 hours' worth of take-home doses of methadone for the first time.",
    "AB 2115 increases the amount of methadone a patient can take home from a clinic.",
    "AB 2115 allows non-methadone clinic doctors to perform federally required physical exams of patients.",
    "AB 2115 removes the requirement for patients to have at least one year of recorded opioid usage before receiving treatment.",
    "AB 2115 eliminates the requirement for patients to participate in frequent counseling services.",
    "AB 2115 allows patients to be absent from treatment for up to 30 days before being removed from a program.",
    "AB 2115 provides physicians with greater discretion to determine appropriate methadone dosages for patients."
  ]
}
```
